extraction.one_line_summary: VLASOV Aleksandr won the 15.8km ITT to Villars by 30+ seconds over GESCHKE Simon, overturning an 18-second deficit to DENNIS Rohan who cracked badly.
tactical_patterns: DENNIS Rohan's pacing strategy collapsed spectacularly after initial time check, losing over 2 minutes despite third-fastest intermediate split.
KRUIJSWIJK Steven's 17-second intermediate advantage demonstrates Jumbo's ability to pace flat TT openings for weaker climbers strategically.
GESCHKE Simon's surprise podium from ninth overall reveals GC vulnerability when pure TT specialists can't sustain climbing power.

rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: First World Tour win via perfectly executed bike change and pacing strategy, gaining 30+ seconds on climb specialists.
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: negative: Third fastest at intermediate but catastrophic fade on climb suggests his TT strength doesn't translate to extended climbing efforts.
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: positive: Only 4 seconds slower than GESCHKE despite age suggests elite climbing ITT capacity developing.
rider_signals.GESCHKE Simon: positive: Jumped from ninth to podium by beating pure climbers in mountain TT, exposing field's time trial weakness.
rider_signals.KRUIJSWIJK Steven: neutral: Strong flat TT split advantage evaporated on climb, confirming limitations in sustained power at altitude.
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Lost podium spot in discipline that should favor young diesel engines, concerning for future stage race aspirations.
